Title: Guard cell-specific glycine decarboxylase manipulation affects Arabidopsis photosynthesis, growth and stomatal behavior

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: H-protein of the glycine decarboxylase complex; lipoate-bearing carrier protein of mitochondrial photorespiration
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Mitochondrial glycine decarboxylation step of the photorespiratory pathway
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Functions as the H-protein carrier subunit of the glycine decarboxylase complex that mediates mitochondrial glycine decarboxylation during photorespiration
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Controls photorespiratory flux that indirectly promotes carbon assimilation and carbohydrate accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key control point for photorespiratory flux
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports plant growth and provides energy/carbon reserves; guard-cell starch supports stomatal movements
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Increased guard-cell GDC-H expression promotes stomatal conductance, photosynthetic CO2 assimilation, carbohydrate accumulation and growth, whereas reduced expression causes the opposite
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Guard cell-specific transgenic overexpression and antisense suppression using the GC1 promoter; RT-PCR; guard-cell/mesophyll immunoblotting; gas exchange; chlorophyll fluorescence; LC-MS/MS and GC metabolite profiling; guard-cell starch microscopy
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Experimentally manipulated specifically in guard cells; unchanged in mesophyll and whole leaf abundance
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Ambient air (400 ppm CO2, 21% O2); elevated CO2 (3000 ppm) suppressing photorespiration; altered O2 (3%, 21%, 40%); different photoperiods
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: PGLP1 (used as calibration control in immunoblots)
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced GDC-H in guard cells decreased growth, photosynthesis, stomatal conductance, transpiration and carbohydrate accumulation; increased stomatal conductance in overexpressors may imply greater water loss, although intrinsic WUE was unchanged
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: LC-MS/MS metabolomics; gas chromatography-based sugar profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Guard cell-specific manipulation of GDC-H changed GDC-H protein abundance in guard cells without altering mesophyll or whole-leaf abundance. 2. Overexpression increased stomatal conductance, transpiration, net CO2 assimilation, carbohydrate accumulation and biomass, while antisense suppression decreased all of these traits. 3. Effects were largely abolished under elevated CO2 or low O2, indicating dependence on photorespiration. 4. Guard-cell starch positively correlated with GDC-H abundance, whereas stomatal density and size were unchanged. 5. GDC-H is therefore implicated as a key photorespiratory determinant of guard-cell metabolism and stomatal behavior.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Guard cell-targeted enhancement of photorespiratory GDC-H is a potential engineering strategy to maintain photosynthesis and growth under conditions requiring high photorespiratory capacity and future climates; guard cell-specific deployment may avoid whole-leaf perturbation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The study manipulated Flaveria pringlei GDC-H transgene expression in Arabidopsis guard cells using both sense and antisense orientations; metabolic effects on carbohydrates are indirect downstream consequences of altered guard-cell photorespiration rather than direct product formation
